text.title: Mobile UX Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every mobile-ux application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Design for the smallest screen first. Identify core content + a single primary action for mobile; add enhancements for tablet and desktop. Never start from desktop and scale down.
rule.rationale: Scaling down a desktop design produces tiny targets, broken layouts, and stripped content. Mobile-first forces honest prioritisation.

rule.statement: One primary action per screen is a HARD constraint. Two equally weighted CTAs on a mobile screen MUST be split or redesigned to establish clear primary/secondary hierarchy.
rule.rationale: Competing actions on small screens cause decision paralysis and lower conversion.

rule.statement: Minimum touch target: 44x44pt (iOS HIG) / 48x48dp (Material). Minimum spacing between adjacent targets: 8px. Hard minimums — not recommendations.

rationale.source: Apple HIG; Material Design accessibility guidance

rule.rationale: Below minimum produces tap errors and accessibility failures.

rule.statement: Place primary actions in the thumb zone (bottom third of screen). Place destructive or rarely needed actions at the top.
rule.rationale: 75% of mobile interaction is one-handed; bottom-zone placement reduces reach error and fatigue.

rule.statement: Use a bottom tab bar (3-5 items) as the primary navigation pattern for mobile apps. Reserve hamburger menus for secondary or overflow navigation only.
rule.rationale: Bottom tabs consistently outperform hamburger menus on discoverability and section awareness.

rule.statement: Set correct input type on all form fields (email / tel / number / search). NEVER disable zoom (user-scalable=no).

rationale.source: MDN — HTML form input types; WCAG 1.4.4

rule.rationale: Correct input types reduce form errors; disabling zoom is an accessibility violation.

rule.statement: Hit Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1, TTI < 5s. Serve WebP with srcset/sizes; lazy-load below-fold content.
rule.rationale: Each 1s delay reduces conversion by ~7%; CLS above 0.1 causes mis-taps when content shifts.

rule.statement: Respect platform-specific gestures (iOS swipe-from-edge back; Android back gesture). NEVER invent non-standard gestures without a visible affordance + button alternative.
rule.rationale: Trained OS gestures work because users learned them from the OS; custom gestures are undiscoverable.

rule.statement: Support VoiceOver / TalkBack. Respect system text-size at 150-200%; never use absolute units that break scaling. Respect prefers-reduced-motion for animations.
rule.rationale: ~15% of users rely on screen readers or system scaling at some point; breaking these excludes them.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
